sections.biography-source-undefined: Dou Wu was a brother to Empress Dowager Dou, who was the Empress to Emperor Huan. When Emperor Ling succeeded the throne after the death of Emperor Huan, many scholars and officials, led by Dou Wu, petitioned for the court to arrest and execute the eunuchs that held sway over the emperor. Surprisingly, Empress Dowager Dou refused this. After this rejection, Dou Wu and the others plotted to remove the eunuchs with force, but the eunuchs discovered this plot before they could actually react. The eunuchs reacted violently by imprisoning Empress Dowager Dou and holding Emperor Ling effectively hostage, then issued false edicts to imprison and kill the plotters, including Dou Wu. Any supporters of him were either imprisoned or also captured and killed.

full_text:
Dou Wu (Youping) 竇武 (游平)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Dou Wu (竇武), styledYouping (游平). Served Emperor Ling of Han as Regent-Marshal. Died opposing the eunuchs. Dou Wu was affiliated with the Han Dynasty. Dou Wu lived during the Later Han Dynasty period of Chinese history.

BiographySource Undefined
Dou Wu was a brother to Empress Dowager Dou, who was the Empress to Emperor Huan. When Emperor Ling succeeded the throne after the death of Emperor Huan, many scholars and officials, led by Dou Wu, petitioned for the court to arrest and execute the eunuchs that held sway over the emperor. Surprisingly, Empress Dowager Dou refused this. After this rejection, Dou Wu and the others plotted to remove the eunuchs with force, but the eunuchs discovered this plot before they could actually react. The eunuchs reacted violently by imprisoning Empress Dowager Dou and holding Emperor Ling effectively hostage, then issued false edicts to imprison and kill the plotters, including Dou Wu. Any supporters of him were either imprisoned or also captured and killed.
